title: Heterotheca oligantha
intro: Heterotheca oligantha, commonly known as bitter goldenrod, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Heterotheca oligantha include sesquiterpene lactones, phenolic compounds, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with bitter goldenrod include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
taxonomy: Heterotheca oligantha is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America. This plant is commonly known as gray false goldeneye. Heterotheca oligantha is a perennial herb, typically growing to a height of around 1-2 feet. It is often found in dry to moist soils in open areas such as prairies and savannas.
uses: Heterotheca oligantha, also known as the narrow-leaved false goldenrod, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. However, limited scientific evidence is available on its specific uses and benefits.
